name: basename
description: Strip directory and suffix from filenames
porting_notes: Minimal port -- 76-line pure C89 program with no Tier 2/3 issues. Transformations: err.h shim, pledge() stubbed, __dead attribute removed, __progname defined directly (weak symbol fix), exit codes changed to AmigaOS values (1->10), amiport_expand_argv() for wildcard expansion, atexit() cleanup for argv. No struct returns, no alignment concerns, no 64-bit types. Note: only handles / as path separator; AmigaOS : volume separator is treated as part of the filename (POSIX behavior). 32/32 FS-UAE tests pass.
known_limitations: Only handles / as path separator. AmigaOS paths with : (e.g. Work:file.txt) are treated as a single filename component. This matches POSIX behavior but differs from what an Amiga-native implementation might do.
readme:
Short:        Strip directory and suffix from filenames
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       The Regents of the University of California (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.14

basename - Strip directory and suffix from filenames

Original author: The Regents of the University of California
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE emulator with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  basename string [suffix]

Examples:
  basename /usr/bin/grep       -> grep
  basename foo.c .c            -> foo
  basename /path/to/file.txt   -> file.txt

Only handles / as path separator (POSIX behavior).
AmigaOS : separator in volume paths is not recognized.

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
